<!--
Thanks for opening a pull request to Theourgia.

Before submitting, please confirm:
- Your commits are attributed to a SAntonopoulou-allowlisted email
  (the pre-push hook and CI both enforce this).
- You have read CONTRIBUTING.md and CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md.
- This PR is not a security vulnerability fix — those go through the
  private security advisory channel first, never as a public PR.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences describing the change and the why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation / planning
- [ ] Infrastructure / tooling
- [ ] Test improvement
- [ ] Other (describe)

## Phase

<!-- Which phase does this change belong to? See PROJECT_PLAN.md §6. -->

- Phase: <!-- e.g., 00 — Foundations -->

## Linked issues

<!-- Closes #N, Refs #N -->

## Tests

- [ ] I have added / updated tests for this change
- [ ] All existing tests still pass locally (`just check`)
- [ ] If this introduces a new feature, the docs (user / admin / developer as applicable) are updated in the same PR

## Documentation & catalog

- [ ] If this change affects user-visible behavior, README / FEATURES.md / CHANGELOG.md are updated
- [ ] If this change affects architecture, an ADR was added or an existing one was superseded
- [ ] If this change affects the phase status (planned → in-progress → done), README roadmap reflects it

## Security / privacy

- [ ] This PR does not introduce telemetry or external network calls outside the documented allowlist
- [ ] No secrets, tokens, or private data are present in the diff
- [ ] Encryption / authentication paths are unchanged, or changes are documented in the PR description

## Tradition-respectful review (if applicable)

- [ ] This change touches tradition-specific content; the affected tradition's practitioners have been consulted, or a `tradition-feedback` issue has been opened for review
- [ ] Not applicable

## Notes for the reviewer

<!-- Anything specific you'd like the reviewer to focus on, push back on, or check. -->
